flashcards.back: A software layer that creates and runs virtual machines, managing hardware resources and providing isolation between VMs.

flashcards.front: Difference between Type 1 and Type 2 hypervisors
flashcards.back: Type 1 runs directly on hardware (bare-metal), Type 2 runs on a host operating system.
Uses CPU extensions (Intel VT-x, AMD-V) to improve virtualization performance and reduce hypervisor overhead.
The process of moving a running VM from one physical host to another without downtime.
A virtualization approach where the guest OS is modified to be aware of the virtualization layer for improved performance.
